trackingsummarydata.subject: CS/econ collabs, all-day Iliad and AI grading
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 03/10/25

urls.page_title: Where computer scientists and economists talk to each other
urls.page_summary: In a world that’s growing more connected every day, economists and computer scientists need to work together. Cornell researchers have thought this way for years, and the rest of the world is catching on.
urls.page_title: Student startup pilots AI grading assistant, joins Y Combinator | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: GradeWiz uses AI and computer vision to grade homework and exams, giving educators more time, the founders say, to devote to teaching.
urls.page_title: Peer recognition crucial for success in physics research | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Even when women receive similar amounts of recognition from peers as men for excelling in physics classes, they perceive significantly less peer recognition, new research has found.
urls.page_title: Joseph Burns, emeritus professor, former dean of faculty, dies at 83 | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Joseph A. Burns, Ph.D. ’66, emeritus professor of engineering and astronomy, and a former vice provost and dean of the Cornell faculty, died Feb. 26 in Ithaca.
urls.page_title: Institutional Genes: The Origins of China’s Institutions and Totalitarianism - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
The Sixth Lecture of the “Unmasking the CCP: History, Politics, and Society in Post-1949 China” Lecture Series
Title: Institutional Genes: The Origins of China’s Institutions and Totalitarianism
Speaker: Prof. Chenggang Xu (Center on China's ...
urls.page_title: ‘Pathways to Peace’ panel to shed light on Middle East conflict | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Former Middle East leaders and ambassadors will hold a wide-ranging public conversation on the historical background and potential paths toward a peaceful future on March 10.
urls.page_title: Chemist Robert Fay, emeritus professor, dies at 88 | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Robert C. Fay, emeritus professor of chemistry and chemical biology in the College of Arts and Sciences, died Feb. 6 in Fairfax, Virginia. He was 88.
urls.page_title: War, love and loyalty: The ‘Iliad’ in Ithaca on March 12-13
urls.page_summary: An event with translator Emily Wilson and a daylong community reading of portions of Homer’s epic poem highlighted the Arts Unplugged event.
Our minds and the ways we tell stories are closely attuned, research shows, and scholar Fritz Breithaupt will explore how that connection works during a March visit as University Lecturer.
urls.page_title: Union Representation - Graduate School : Graduate School
urls.page_summary: Cornell Graduate Student Workers United – United Electrical, Radio and Machine Workers of America (CGSU-UE) is the exclusive representative of graduate student workers for the purposes of collective bargaining. The…
